ADCs within iPDC/icPDC

K√ľrŇüat Birgin
Meeting, 06.10.2025

1

Rationale behind our ADC selection

List of ADCs from Selleckchem (and also MedChemExpress)

First filtered the list for relevance to HGSC

Then further narrowed by usability for icPDCs

Rationale behind our ADC selection

And thanks to the knowledge of Anniina

Removed the ‚ÄúAvoid‚ÄĚ entries

Then filtered for ADCs that are currently in Clinical Trials

Yellowed marked ADCs got selected

In case someone is interested

Possible experimental controls

5

Aino‚Äôs (Thesis) gating strategy:

Exclude debris: Gate out small particles or cell fragments that are not relevant to the analysis.

Select single cells: Ensure only individual cells are analyzed, excluding doublets or aggregates.

Gate live cells: Exclude dead cells using the live/dead marker.

Identify major populations using:

EpCAM‚Āļ ‚Üí Cancer cells
CD45‚Āļ ‚Üí Leukocytes
EpCAM‚ĀĽ CD45‚ĀĽ ‚Üí Non-immune stroma
EpCAM- CD45- CD31+ ‚Üí Endothelial cells
EpCAM- CD45- CD31- FAP+/a-SMA+/PDRGF-b+/CD29+ ‚Üí Cancer-associated fibroblasts (CAFs)

6

Possible ADC readout: Binding assay

With therapeutic antibodies gating is straightforward, but not with ADCs ‚Ä¶ so, we could use:

7

e.g., Alexa Fluor 647 or PerCP-Cy5.5

Note: Secondary antibody should not bind anything else, especially Fc receptors on immune cells. So, we may need an Fc block to prevent nonspecific binding (which we already have, I think).

Possible ADC readout: Toxicity assay

Tumor cells should be the primary ADC target

But ADCs may cause a bystander effect, where the payload diffuses into nearby non-target cells (CAF, immune cells), so we want to detect also cell death/apoptosis 

Inside each population, we could gate for:

X axis: Annexin V (e.g., AF700) (Note: Annexin V Binding Buffer (contains Ca¬≤‚Āļ))
Y axis: DCM (viability dye)

And then interpret Quadrants as:

Annexin‚ąí / DCM‚ąí ‚Üí Live (healthy)
Annexin+ / DCM‚ąí ‚Üí Early apoptotic
Annexin+ / DCM+ ‚Üí Late apoptotic / necrotic
Annexin‚ąí / DCM+ ‚Üí Necrotic / membrane-compromised

8

Issue: additional titration needed for

9

Unconjugated Antibody

Median Fluorescence Intensity (MFI) increase in EpCAM+ cells (tumor cells) vs EpCAM‚ąí (CAF, immune) tells you your unconjugated antibody is binding specifically.

Fluorescent Anti-Human IgG

MFI on EpCAM+ (tumor cells) vs secondary-only (background control) vs EpCAM‚ąí/CD45+ (immune cells)

Fc-block

MFI of CD45+ should drop after blocking, as Fc receptors are only on immune cells

Thank you. Open for input! ‚ėļÔłŹ

10

08.10.2025

11

Suggestion titration concentration

Datopotamab deruxtecan

Plot A: KR(CH)31 is¬†a human epithelial ovarian cancer cell

Plot B-D: OVA is a high grade serous ovarian cancer cell line 


Notation: 

3+ ‚Üí High expression on the cell surface
2+ ‚Üí Medium/Moderate expression
1+ ‚Üí Low expression
0 ‚Üí No detectable expression

https://doi.org/10.1016/j.ygyno.2024.07.002

Suggestion titration concentration

Datopotamab deruxtecan

Plot A (SARARK9): uterine carcinosarcoma
 
Plot B (SARARK3): ovarian carcinosarcoma 

Plot C (SARARK1): uterine serous carcinoma 

Plot D (SARARK14): uterine serous carcinoma

https://doi.org/10.1016/j.ygyno.2025.06.017

Suggestion titration concentration

Datopotamab deruxtecan

Plot A-C: END(K) is¬†a cell line to represent poorly differentiated endometrial carcinoma

doi: 10.1158/2767-9764.CRC-25-0251

Suggestion titration concentration

Sacituzumab tirumotecan

No supportive publications available 

But DXd (payload of Datopotamab deruxtecan) is ‚ąľ10 times more potent than SN-38 (payload of Sacituzumab govitecan)

The 0.01‚Äď1‚ÄĮ¬Ķg/mL titration range spans a 10-fold window, which should capture the biologically relevant concentrations even if the payload of Sacituzumab tirumotecan (KL610023) is slightly more or less potent than SN-38, making it reasonable to apply the same range for Sacituzumab tirumotecan.

https://doi.org/10.1016/j.annonc.2019.11.019

Thank you. Open for input! ‚ėļÔłŹ
